forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTCAEXMCAWWTE7FBGP7BE86Z
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8480'
+project: card-comments
+title: Route comment ops through dispatch, register in schema, and add command YAML
+---
+## What
+Wire the comment command structs into the op dispatch + schema so `{"op": "add comment", ...}`, `list comments`, `get comment`, `update comment`, `delete comment` are reachable end to end. The `Noun::Comment`/`Comments` enum entries and `is_valid` (verb,noun) pairs ALREADY EXIST in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/types/operation.rs` (lines ~96-97, ~144-145, ~281-283) — do NOT re-add them; this task connects them to the implementations.
+
+Files:
+1. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs`:
+   - Add `async fn execute_comment_operation(processor, ctx, op)` modeled on `execute_attachment_operation` (~line 849). Map `Verb::Add → AddComment`, `Verb::List → ListComments`, `Verb::Get → GetComment`, `Verb::Update → UpdateComment`, `Verb::Delete → DeleteComment`. Use the same `req`/`req_task_id`/`op.get_string` helpers. The actor for `add comment` should default to the op's actor (`op.actor`) when not explicitly passed — check how `execute_operation` derives `processor` from `op.actor` and reuse that as the comment author when `actor` param is absent.
+   - Add the match arm to `execute_operation`'s `match op.noun` (~line 928): `Noun::Comment | Noun::Comments => execute_comment_operation(&processor, ctx, op).await,` — currently it falls through to the `_ => unsupported operation` arm.
+2. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/schema.rs`:
+   - Import the five comment structs and add them to the `KANBAN_OPERATIONS` static list (in a `// Comment` group, mirroring the `// Attachment` group at ~line 67).
+   - Add an example to `generate_kanban_examples()` (e.g. add comment to a task).
+3. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/comment.yaml` — new command YAML modeled on `builtin/commands/attachment.yaml` (context-menu entries for comment actions if appropriate; at minimum keep it consistent with how attachment.yaml registers commands). Confirm command YAMLs are dynamically loaded (include_dir) so the new file is picked up.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `execute_operation` routes `Noun::Comment | Noun::Comments` to the comment handler (no longer "unsupported operation").
+- [ ] All five comment ops appear in the generated MCP schema `op` enum and in `x-operation-schemas`.
+- [ ] An `add comment` example appears in the schema examples.
+- [ ] `add comment` with no explicit `actor` records the dispatching actor as author.
+- [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -- -D warnings` clean.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] In `dispatch.rs` test module: parse + execute `{"op":"add comment","task_id":...,"text":"hi"}` end to end against a temp board, then `{"op":"list comments","task_id":...}` returns the member. Assert round-trip through `parse_input` → `execute_operation`.
+- [ ] In `schema.rs` test module: extend the existing `test_kanban_operations_returns_full_list`-style assertions to require `add comment`, `list comments`, `get comment`, `update comment`, `delete comment` in the op enum, and `x-operation-schemas` count matches ops.
+- [ ] Test that `add comment` without an `actor` param attributes the dispatching actor.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` — green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the dispatch round-trip + schema-enum assertions first; they fail until routing/registration is added.
\ No newline at end of file

-## What

-
-Files:
-1. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs`:
-   - Add `async fn execute_comment_operation(processor, ctx, op)` modeled on `execute_attachment_operation` (~line 849). Map `Verb::Add → AddComment`, `Verb::List → ListComments`, `Verb::Get → GetComment`, `Verb::Update → UpdateComment`, `Verb::Delete → DeleteComment`. Use the same `req`/`req_task_id`/`op.get_string` helpers. The actor for `add comment` should default to the op's actor (`op.actor`) when not explicitly passed — check how `execute_operation` derives `processor` from `op.actor` and reuse that as the comment author when `actor` param is absent.
-   - Add the match arm to `execute_operation`'s `match op.noun` (~line 928): `Noun::Comment | Noun::Comments => execute_comment_operation(&processor, ctx, op).await,` — currently it falls through to the `_ => unsupported operation` arm.
-2. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/schema.rs`:
-   - Import the five comment structs and add them to the `KANBAN_OPERATIONS` static list (in a `// Comment` group, mirroring the `// Attachment` group at ~line 67).
-   - Add an example to `generate_kanban_examples()` (e.g. add comment to a task).
-3. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/comment.yaml` — new command YAML modeled on `builtin/commands/attachment.yaml` (context-menu entries for comment actions if appropriate; at minimum keep it consistent with how attachment.yaml registers commands). Confirm command YAMLs are dynamically loaded (include_dir) so the new file is picked up.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `execute_operation` routes `Noun::Comment | Noun::Comments` to the comment handler (no longer "unsupported operation").
-- [ ] All five comment ops appear in the generated MCP schema `op` enum and in `x-operation-schemas`.
-- [ ] An `add comment` example appears in the schema examples.
-- [ ] `add comment` with no explicit `actor` records the dispatching actor as author.
-- [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -- -D warnings` clean.
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] In `dispatch.rs` test module: parse + execute `{"op":"add comment","task_id":...,"text":"hi"}` end to end against a temp board, then `{"op":"list comments","task_id":...}` returns the member. Assert round-trip through `parse_input` → `execute_operation`.
-- [ ] In `schema.rs` test module: extend the existing `test_kanban_operations_returns_full_list`-style assertions to require `add comment`, `list comments`, `get comment`, `update comment`, `delete comment` in the op enum, and `x-operation-schemas` count matches ops.
-- [ ] Test that `add comment` without an `actor` param attributes the dispatching actor.
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` — green.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the dispatch round-trip + schema-enum assertions first; they fail until routing/registration is added.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -13,7 +13,8 @@

 Files:
 1. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs`:
-   - Add `async fn execute_comment_operation(processor, ctx, op)` modeled on `execute_attachment_operation` (~line 849). Map `Verb::Add → AddComment`, `Verb::List → ListComments`, `Verb::Get → GetComment`, `Verb::Update → UpdateComment`, `Verb::Delete → DeleteComment`. Use the same `req`/`req_task_id`/`op.get_string` helpers. The actor for `add comment` should default to the op's actor (`op.actor`) when not explicitly passed — check how `execute_operation` derives `processor` from `op.actor` and reuse that as the comment author when `actor` param is absent.
+   - Add `async fn execute_comment_operation(processor, ctx, op)` modeled on `execute_attachment_operation` (~line 849). Map `Verb::Add → AddComment`, `Verb::List → ListComments`, `Verb::Get → GetComment`, `Verb::Update → UpdateComment`, `Verb::Delete → DeleteComment`. Use the same `req`/`req_task_id`/`op.get_string` helpers.
+   - **Author resolution for `add comment`** (design decision: fallback local actor): when an explicit `actor` param is passed, use it; else use the op's dispatching actor (`op.actor`); else, when neither resolves (e.g. a human in the UI with no actor configured), fall back to a default/local actor so `add comment` NEVER errors for a missing author. Implement the fallback as a single helper (e.g. resolve_comment_author) and ensure the local/default actor exists or is synthesized consistently with how the rest of the system represents an unconfigured local user. Confirm whether a canonical "local"/default actor id already exists before inventing one.
    - Add the match arm to `execute_operation`'s `match op.noun` (~line 928): `Noun::Comment | Noun::Comments => execute_comment_operation(&processor, ctx, op).await,` — currently it falls through to the `_ => unsupported operation` arm.
 2. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/schema.rs`:
    - Import the five comment structs and add them to the `KANBAN_OPERATIONS` static list (in a `// Comment` group, mirroring the `// Attachment` group at ~line 67).
@@ -24,14 +25,14 @@
 - [ ] `execute_operation` routes `Noun::Comment | Noun::Comments` to the comment handler (no longer "unsupported operation").
 - [ ] All five comment ops appear in the generated MCP schema `op` enum and in `x-operation-schemas`.
 - [ ] An `add comment` example appears in the schema examples.
-- [ ] `add comment` with no explicit `actor` records the dispatching actor as author.
+- [ ] `add comment` with an explicit `actor` uses it; with no `actor` but a dispatching actor uses that; with neither, falls back to a default/local actor and still succeeds (never errors on missing author).
 - [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -- -D warnings` clean.

 ## Tests
 - [ ] In `dispatch.rs` test module: parse + execute `{"op":"add comment","task_id":...,"text":"hi"}` end to end against a temp board, then `{"op":"list comments","task_id":...}` returns the member. Assert round-trip through `parse_input` → `execute_operation`.
 - [ ] In `schema.rs` test module: extend the existing `test_kanban_operations_returns_full_list`-style assertions to require `add comment`, `list comments`, `get comment`, `update comment`, `delete comment` in the op enum, and `x-operation-schemas` count matches ops.
-- [ ] Test that `add comment` without an `actor` param attributes the dispatching actor.
+- [ ] Author-fallback tests: (a) explicit actor honored; (b) no actor param but dispatching actor present → that actor recorded; (c) neither present → default/local actor recorded, op succeeds.
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` — green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the dispatch round-trip + schema-enum assertions first; they fail until routing/registration is added.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the dispatch round-trip + schema-enum + author-fallback assertions first; they fail until routing/registration is added.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -14,25 +14,25 @@
 Files:
 1. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs`:
    - Add `async fn execute_comment_operation(processor, ctx, op)` modeled on `execute_attachment_operation` (~line 849). Map `Verb::Add → AddComment`, `Verb::List → ListComments`, `Verb::Get → GetComment`, `Verb::Update → UpdateComment`, `Verb::Delete → DeleteComment`. Use the same `req`/`req_task_id`/`op.get_string` helpers.
-   - **Author resolution for `add comment`** (design decision: fallback local actor): when an explicit `actor` param is passed, use it; else use the op's dispatching actor (`op.actor`); else, when neither resolves (e.g. a human in the UI with no actor configured), fall back to a default/local actor so `add comment` NEVER errors for a missing author. Implement the fallback as a single helper (e.g. resolve_comment_author) and ensure the local/default actor exists or is synthesized consistently with how the rest of the system represents an unconfigured local user. Confirm whether a canonical "local"/default actor id already exists before inventing one.
+   - **Author: pass-through, NOT resolved here.** Construct `AddComment { task_id, actor: <explicit actor param if present, else op.actor (the dispatching actor), as Option<String>>, text }`. Do NOT implement fallback logic in dispatch — `AddComment::execute()` owns author resolution (explicit→validate, None→OS-user fallback) per the dependency task. Dispatch only forwards whatever actor it has (which may be None).
    - Add the match arm to `execute_operation`'s `match op.noun` (~line 928): `Noun::Comment | Noun::Comments => execute_comment_operation(&processor, ctx, op).await,` — currently it falls through to the `_ => unsupported operation` arm.
 2. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/schema.rs`:
    - Import the five comment structs and add them to the `KANBAN_OPERATIONS` static list (in a `// Comment` group, mirroring the `// Attachment` group at ~line 67).
    - Add an example to `generate_kanban_examples()` (e.g. add comment to a task).
-3. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/comment.yaml` — new command YAML modeled on `builtin/commands/attachment.yaml` (context-menu entries for comment actions if appropriate; at minimum keep it consistent with how attachment.yaml registers commands). Confirm command YAMLs are dynamically loaded (include_dir) so the new file is picked up.
+3. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/comment.yaml` — new command YAML modeled on `builtin/commands/attachment.yaml` (context-menu entries for comment actions if appropriate; at minimum keep it consistent with how attachment.yaml registers commands). CONFIRMED command YAMLs are loaded via `include_dir!` (`src/lib.rs:125` BUILTIN_COMMANDS) so the new file is picked up with no Rust list edit.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `execute_operation` routes `Noun::Comment | Noun::Comments` to the comment handler (no longer "unsupported operation").
 - [ ] All five comment ops appear in the generated MCP schema `op` enum and in `x-operation-schemas`.
 - [ ] An `add comment` example appears in the schema examples.
-- [ ] `add comment` with an explicit `actor` uses it; with no `actor` but a dispatching actor uses that; with neither, falls back to a default/local actor and still succeeds (never errors on missing author).
+- [ ] `add comment` dispatched with a dispatching actor records that actor; dispatched with none still succeeds (OS-user fallback handled in `AddComment::execute`, not dispatch).
 - [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -- -D warnings` clean.

 ## Tests
 - [ ] In `dispatch.rs` test module: parse + execute `{"op":"add comment","task_id":...,"text":"hi"}` end to end against a temp board, then `{"op":"list comments","task_id":...}` returns the member. Assert round-trip through `parse_input` → `execute_operation`.
 - [ ] In `schema.rs` test module: extend the existing `test_kanban_operations_returns_full_list`-style assertions to require `add comment`, `list comments`, `get comment`, `update comment`, `delete comment` in the op enum, and `x-operation-schemas` count matches ops.
-- [ ] Author-fallback tests: (a) explicit actor honored; (b) no actor param but dispatching actor present → that actor recorded; (c) neither present → default/local actor recorded, op succeeds.
+- [ ] Dispatch with an explicit dispatching actor attributes that actor on the resulting member.
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` — green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the dispatch round-trip + schema-enum + author-fallback assertions first; they fail until routing/registration is added.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the dispatch round-trip + schema-enum assertions first; they fail until routing/registration is added.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,33 +6,35 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '8480'
 project: card-comments
-title: Route comment ops through dispatch, register in schema, and add command YAML
+title: Route comment ops through dispatch and register in schema (agent-facing)
 ---
 ## What
-Wire the comment command structs into the op dispatch + schema so `{"op": "add comment", ...}`, `list comments`, `get comment`, `update comment`, `delete comment` are reachable end to end. The `Noun::Comment`/`Comments` enum entries and `is_valid` (verb,noun) pairs ALREADY EXIST in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/types/operation.rs` (lines ~96-97, ~144-145, ~281-283) — do NOT re-add them; this task connects them to the implementations.
+Wire the comment command structs into the kanban **op** dispatch + schema so the MCP agent can call `{"op": "add comment", ...}`, `list comments`, `get comment`, `update comment`, `delete comment` end to end. (This is the agent-facing path. The UI does NOT use these ops — it uses the generic `entity.update_field` field-set path, handled in a separate task.) The `Noun::Comment`/`Comments` enum entries and `is_valid` (verb,noun) pairs ALREADY EXIST in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/types/operation.rs` (lines ~96-97, ~144-145, ~281-283) — do NOT re-add them; this task connects them to the implementations.
+
+NOTE: No `comment.yaml` command file and no `register_commands` changes are needed. CONFIRMED via research that the UI mutates fields through the generic `entity.update_field` command (editors are pure UI); comments use that path, so there is no `comment.add`/`update`/`delete` UI command. This task is purely the kanban-op (agent) surface.

 Files:
 1. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs`:
    - Add `async fn execute_comment_operation(processor, ctx, op)` modeled on `execute_attachment_operation` (~line 849). Map `Verb::Add → AddComment`, `Verb::List → ListComments`, `Verb::Get → GetComment`, `Verb::Update → UpdateComment`, `Verb::Delete → DeleteComment`. Use the same `req`/`req_task_id`/`op.get_string` helpers.
-   - **Author: pass-through, NOT resolved here.** Construct `AddComment { task_id, actor: <explicit actor param if present, else op.actor (the dispatching actor), as Option<String>>, text }`. Do NOT implement fallback logic in dispatch — `AddComment::execute()` owns author resolution (explicit→validate, None→OS-user fallback) per the dependency task. Dispatch only forwards whatever actor it has (which may be None).
-   - Add the match arm to `execute_operation`'s `match op.noun` (~line 928): `Noun::Comment | Noun::Comments => execute_comment_operation(&processor, ctx, op).await,` — currently it falls through to the `_ => unsupported operation` arm.

Registering the five ops in `KANBAN_OPERATIONS` automatically lands them on BOTH surfaces — but tests must assert against the right surface (see below).
+
 Files:
 1. `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs`:
    - Add `async fn execute_comment_operation(processor, ctx, op)` modeled on `execute_attachment_operation` (~line 849).  ## Acceptance Criteria
